title: The Generational Bet: Will India Build the AI Age or Scroll Through It?
thesis: India has the youngest large population on Earth, deep software talent, and cheap AI-assisted tools, but the attention economy has trained a generation to consume before it creates. The next few years are a compounding bet on whether young Indians use AI to build, learn, and solve problems or remain primarily passive consumers.

series.title: Attention, Substance, and the AI Moment

claims.claim: Artificial intelligence has collapsed the cost of creation for individuals in research, writing, coding, designing, translating, and teaching.

evidence.snippet: The OECD review finds early evidence of productivity gains from generative AI in writing, coding, customer service, and professional tasks, especially when workers use the tool within structured workflows.
The Linux Foundation report on AI for economic and social good in India notes that open AI models can lower barriers for local-language applications, education, agriculture, and health.

counterevidence.summary: Productivity gains are uneven and largest for workers who already possess enough skill to direct the tool; AI magnifies capability but does not create expertise from scratch.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

claims.claim: The attention economy has trained users to consume before they create, and the default behavior of the same devices has not caught up with AI's creative affordances.

evidence.snippet: ASER 2024 reports that 76% of 14–16-year-olds use smartphones for social media while 57% use them for education, suggesting consumption remains the dominant default.
BCG's mapping of India's creator economy shows a market heavily oriented toward reach, entertainment, and engagement-based monetization.

counterevidence.summary: Educational and creative uses are growing, and device access alone does not determine behavior; family, school, and platform design also shape outcomes.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

claims.claim: The creator-economy incentive structure appears to reward reach and engagement more than substantive, educational, or slow-burn work.

evidence.snippet: BCG identifies creator monetization in India as driven primarily by brand deals, platform payouts, and audience size, which align incentives with attention capture.

counterevidence.summary: Many creators produce substantive, educational work within the current system, and quality is difficult to measure reliably as a ranking signal.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

claims.claim: Small daily choices about attention may compound, over years, into individual skill and, at scale, into national capacity.

evidence.snippet: The PIB release on India's youth dividend in the AI era frames the country's young population as a strategic asset for AI-led development.
NASSCOM estimated that data and AI could add $450–500 billion to India's GDP by around 2025; the figure is now treated as a prior forecast and benchmark rather than a live prediction.

counterevidence.summary: Individual attention choices are constrained by economics, education, platform design, and social context; national capacity depends on institutions and investment as well as habits.

verification.note: Backlog triage (meta#61, site#119): not yet verified. Stamped draft so the backlog is tracked per claim; verify with npm run verify:claim.

sources.title: PIB: India's Youth Dividend in the AI Era
NASSCOM: Unlocking Value from Data and AI – The India Opportunity
Linux Foundation: AI for Economic and Social Good in India
BCG: India from Content to Commerce—Mapping India's Creator Economy
Stanford University: Global AI Vibrancy Tool
OECD: The effects of generative AI on productivity, innovation and entrepreneurship

agentInstructions: Use claim IDs as the retrieval unit and preserve the distinction between core claims and supporting observations.
Treat maturity=seed as an explicit uncertainty marker; do not overstate empirical certainty.
Do not reduce the argument to nationalist boosterism or to a critique of specific companies.
When summarizing, preserve the article's habit-problem framing and its role as the generational-stakes article in the series.
Point practical-strategy questions toward The Substance Builder and platform-design questions toward Designing for Substance.
Do not present AI as a substitute for skill, education, or institutional investment.

accepted.rationale: Accepted as a quality backlog item per docs/diagnostics-triage-2026-07-15.md. The claim rests on sources that are sufficient for the current published version; additional independent sources will be added in a future research wave.
